• Make fast revisions when needed

• Study our current thumbnails and improve upon them

• Collaborate as part of a team and follow direction carefully

• Be responsive and professional in communication



???? What We Are Looking For

• Advanced Adobe Photoshop skills (non-negotiable)

• Strong cutout skills, lighting adjustments, color grading, and compositing

• Deep understanding of YouTube thumbnail psychology

• Ability to create emotional intensity and strong visual hierarchy

• Understanding of what drives high CTR on YouTube

• Fast turnaround and ability to work under deadlines

• Reliable communication and team-oriented mindset

• Hungry, proactive, and growth-focused attitude



???? Compensation

Starting Pay: $600 USD per month

This includes:

• Up to 25 thumbnails per month

• 12- 24 hour turnaround expectation

• 1–2 revision rounds per thumbnail while still meeting daily requirements.

Additional thumbnails beyond 25 will be compensated separately.

This is a dedicated part-time retainer position. You will be expected to prioritize our projects when assigned.

Compensation increases are performance-based.

???? 60–90 Day Assessment Period

All new hires will begin with a 60–90 day assessment period. During this time, we will evaluate:

• Quality of thumbnail designs

• Consistency of turnaround time

• Ability to follow direction

• Communication and teamwork

• Initiative and creative improvement

• Overall packaging performance

At the end of this period, we will determine long-term fit and discuss potential growth opportunities based on performance.



???? Application Requirements (READ CAREFULLY)

To be considered, you MUST:

Include the word “CTR” in your application

Send 2–3 YouTube thumbnails you personally created

Briefly explain what makes a YouTube thumbnail high-performing

Confirm you are willing to complete a sample thumbnail design as part of the interview process (UNPAID) To test your thumbnail abilities.

Confirm you are open to a video interview if shortlisted

Applications that do not follow instructions will not be considered.



NOTE: Sample Thumbnail Test IS REQUIRED FOR ALL APPLICANTS 

Shortlisted candidates will be asked to complete a sample thumbnail design to evaluate skill level, creativity, ability to follow direction, and understanding of our channel style.



???? Important

• All designs created under this role are company property

• Must be comfortable signing a basic agreement

• Must maintain confidentiality

• Must not reuse designs for competitors in the same niche

We are building long-term.

We are serious about performance.

We are looking for someone who is talented, disciplined, understands human psychology, and is ready to grow with a fast-scaling YouTube channel.

If that’s you — APPLY.

title: Google Ads Specialist, Fix Tracking + Generate Qualified Leads (Project-Based)
description:
We are travel and accommodation agency working with B2B clients across Europe.



We are currently running Google Ads but the account has poor tracking and unreliable conversion data. We are looking for a specialist to fix the setup and deliver measurable, real leads.



This is a short, project-based role with clear deliverables. If results are strong, this can turn into ongoing work.



CURRENT SITUATION (read carefully)

• Google Ads shows 7,000+ form submissions that are not real

• Page views and other events are counted as conversions

• Tracking is set up via GTM with custom events and likely firing incorrectly

• Campaigns have been running for months with no reliable cost per lead

If you don’t immediately understand what’s wrong here, do not apply.



YOUR JOB

1. Audit and fix conversion tracking

 • Only count real form submissions and qualified calls

 • Remove all fake or duplicate events

 • Ensure 1 real submission = 1 tracked conversion

2. Rebuild or clean campaign structure

 • Focus on high-intent B2B search terms

 • Remove low-quality traffic sources

 • Implement proper negative keywords

3. Validate tracking

 • Test with real submissions

 • Match Google Ads data with actual leads 
/CRM)

4. Launch and optimize

 • Generate first verified leads

 • Show real cost per lead



DELIVERABLES

• Clean, verified conversion tracking (no inflated numbers)

• 1–3 focused search campaigns

• Keyword + negative keyword structure

• Proof of real leads generated

• Clear cost per lead



PAYMENT

Project: $150–300

Bonus if you generate qualified leads at a strong cost



REQUIRED TO APPLY (no exceptions)

Answer these clearly and practically:

 1. A Google Ads account shows 7,000 form submissions, but the business received almost none. What is the most likely cause and how do you fix it?

 2. How do you ensure a form submission is counted only once and only when it is actually completed?

 3. When using Google Tag Manager, what would you check if a conversion event is firing too often?

 4. Would you ever track page views or button clicks as primary conversions in lead generation? Why or why not?

Keep answers short and technical.

title: Inventory & Invoice Reconciliation Specialist – Remote
description:
We are a U.S.-based wholesale food distribution company looking for someone who can improve the way our business operates.



This is not a data entry job,

We are hiring operators who think with data



We are looking for someone who can analyze operational data, identify problems, and design clean, reusable reporting systems that help management make decisions quickly.



The right person thinks like an architect, not an operator.



You should enjoy working with messy datasets and transforming them into structured systems that reveal operational and financial problems.



?



WHAT YOU WILL WORK WITH



Operational datasets such as:



* purchase orders

* vendor invoices

* inventory reports

* reconciliation reports

* margin analysis reports



Your role is to build models that quickly answer questions like:



* What went wrong?

* Where is the financial exposure?

* Which items or vendors caused the problem?



A good report should allow management to understand the issue in under two minutes.



?



hiring operators who think with data



WHO SHOULD APPLY



You will likely succeed in this role if you:



* enjoy analyzing messy datasets

* prefer building systems instead of manual lists

* design formula-driven models that update automatically

* structure reports so they are easy to audit and review

* think about financial impact, not just item counts





ASSESSMENT



To apply, complete the short analysis task below.



Download the assessment file.



The workbook contains two sheets:



Sheet 1 – Purchase Order

Sheet 2 – Vendor Bill



Your task is to analyze the differences between the two documents.



Identify:



* items missing from the vendor bill

* items added by the vendor

* price differences



Present the findings in a clear format that management can review quickly and audit.



Your submission should show how you think, structure analysis, and present operational problems.



Assessment file:







?



WORK STRUCTURE



Remote –



Schedule:

Monday – Saturday

8 hours per day



APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS

	1.	Download the assessment file

	2.	Complete the analysis

	3.	Submit your finished Excel workbook in .xlsx format



You may organize your workbook in any structure (single sheet or multiple sheets).



Do not include explanations in the
.

All logic should be shown inside the Excel file.



Do not delete or modify the original sheets. All sheets must be preserved so the work can be audited.

Do not convert formulas to values. The file must remain fully auditable.



Only candidates who submit the completed assessment will be reviewed.



 your submission in .xlsx format to:







Subject: Assessment



Attach the Excel file only.

Do not include any text in the body of the
.

title: Marketing + Sales Support VA
description:
SALES & GROWTH MANAGER - FOXSTAYS



We’re looking for a highly driven Sales & Growth Manager to help grow FoxStays through outbound sales, lead conversion, client communication, and strategic partnerships.



This is a sales-first role. Around 80-90% of the work is focused on outreach, follow-up, deal progression, and closing. The remaining 10-20% includes light marketing support.



In this role, you will cold call homeowners, dock owners, brokers, realtors, yacht agents, and potential partners. You will also reach out through phone,
, WhatsApp, and social media to generate leads, qualify opportunities, book meetings, and help move deals forward.



You will respond to inbound inquiries, call leads quickly, follow up consistently, and keep opportunities organized through every stage of the pipeline.



You will also contact FoxStays guests to offer approved add-on services such as rental cars, boat trips, and local experiences in order to increase revenue from existing bookings.



You will help build relationships with brokers, dock owners, realtors, and local business partners, and support new referral and partnership opportunities.



You will maintain the CRM by tracking leads, conversations, follow-ups, and deal stages, and by keeping the pipeline updated and organized.



You may also support light marketing tasks such as posting on social media, replying to comments and messages, engaging in relevant
 groups, and creating simple Canva content when needed.



Requirements:

Strong spoken and written English

Comfortable with daily calls and outreach

Sales, business development, or customer-facing experience

Strong follow-up habits and confidence handling objections

Organized, proactive, and persistent

CRM experience is a plus

Experience in hospitality, real estate, luxury service, yachting, or partnerships is a plus



KPIs:

Call and outreach volume

Response speed

Follow-ups completed

Meetings booked

Deals closed

Revenue generated



Compensation:

Base salary

Commission

Performance bonus



This role is for someone who is confident, organized, persistent, and motivated by results.



To apply, please send a short introduction about yourself, your relevant experience, and a voice recording in English.

title: Online Community Research Assistant (Community Platforms / Social Media)
description:
We are looking for someone who understands how niche online communities and social platforms work (Reddit, Discord,
/X, YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, forums).



Your job is not marketing or sales.

Your job is to find people actively discussing certain problems online and invite them to give honest feedback on a project we are testing.

We are trying to understand how real users react to the platform.



The Goal

The goal is not to promote the platform.

The goal is to collect honest feedback from real people who are currently experiencing the problem.

Your role is to interact naturally with those users and invite them to briefly test the platform and share their genuine opinion.



Your daily tasks will include:

Identifying people actively discussing problems such as:

Doomscrolling

Algorithm fatigue

Difficulty finding useful content online

Participating naturally in discussions.

Inviting some users to briefly test the project and share honest feedback.

Collecting real user reactions and reporting them.



You may operate on any platform where real conversations are happening, including but not limited to:

Reddit

Discord


 / X

YouTube comments

TikTok comments

Instagram comments

Forums or niche communities



The important factor is real conversations happening in real time, not the platform itself.

For example, if a video or post has many people complaining about doomscrolling, algorithm fatigue, or the difficulty of finding useful content, you may engage in that discussion naturally and invite interested users to briefly test the platform.



Expected Daily Output

Generate 5–10 real visits to the platform through honest, non-spammy invitations.

Deliver 4–5 real user insights (feedback)



Examples of insights we value:

“This is confusing because…”

“I would actually use this if it had…”

“This feels like another useless directory…”



Each insight should include:

Link to the conversation or post

Screenshot (only if a public link is not available)

Short summary of the feedback



You should:

Be comfortable navigating online communities and social platforms

Understand community etiquette and how to participate naturally

Have strong written English

Be able to detect sarcasm, frustration, and tone in conversations



This role is not spam or promotion.

This role requires natural participation in conversations.

If your messages look like marketing, communities will reject them.



Important Rules

Never:

Spam links

Copy/paste the same message

Start conversations by promoting the project

First engage naturally. Only invite people when it makes sense.



Trial Period

This role will start with a paid trial (1 week).

Typical time commitment:

2–3 hours per day

If the collaboration works well, we can continue long-term.



When applying, send me:

A Reddit thread (or any public post) where someone is complaining about doomscrolling, algorithm feeds, or difficulty finding useful content.

Write the response you would post to that person.



This helps us understand how you interact naturally in online communities.

title: Senior Tax Accountant
description:
At About Profit, we are ALL about advising small business clients on maximizing their profitability. We are one of only 60 Profit First Mastery Certified Firms in the United States. That means we are the go-to experts in cash management and profitability for savvy small business owners who have cash in the bank to correlate to their profitability.



Even though our team stretches across the country, we work with a Staten Island hustle and a Brooklyn grind that our clients love.



Our team is growing! We need a Senior Accountant.



About Profit's Core Values



No A**holes: We promote positive interactions and are free from toxic behavior.

Friendly: We create a welcoming and approachable work environment.

Personable: We are genuine and create relatable interactions within the workplace.







Key Responsibilities 



#1. Complete clean-up for new clients - including fixing issues, reconciling accounts, completing client notes, and creating the REVIEW SHEET for the client's financials. 

Measure of Success: New clients' books are ready for bookkeeping staff to begin ongoing monthly work within seven business days of initiating client onboarding 



#2. Reply to customer
s quickly and efficiently. 

Measure of Success: 100% of
s are acknowledged during the same business day with a thoughtful and helpful reply. 90% of client requests do not incur a second request/-inquiry from the client because their initial request was sufficiently fulfilled.



#3. Tee-up tax returns for Tax Managers to complete by reviewing clients' accounting files, reconciling balances to end-of-year bank account statements, flagging and getting resolution on any issues that would impact the accuracy of data in preparing the return, and completing data entry into the tax software.

Measure of Success: Complete daily assigned tax work in the budgeted time  allotted for each work card in Carbon (project management software)



#4. Pull and review Monthly Financial Statements for assigned full-service bookkeeping and tax accounting clients, including: 

Annual Comparison

% of Income Comparison by Month

Cross-check against clients' other applicable software/systems that feed or are reflected in their financials

Review balance sheet and corresponding accounts such as Sales Tax, Payroll, A/R, A/P, Inventory, etc.

Measure of Success: Provide feedback to bookkeeping staff to ensure 10% or less rate of recurring errors. 



#5. Proactively identify the "out of the normal" exceptions, errors, and opportunities for improvement - especially when backing up Junior Accountant 

Measure of Success: Complete regular, monthly 1:1 meetings with individual bookkeepers and interns - including providing written notes highlighting at least five learning and/or problem-solving points with each bookkeeper or intern



Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Accounting or a related field

At least 3 years of US bookkeeping experience with a US accounting firm

Required proficiency in QuickBooks Desktop (QBD) and QuickBooks Online (QBO)

Strong communication skills, both written and verbal

Tech-savvy, with proficiency in tools such as Excel and automation applications.

title: Paid Media Manager
description:
Paid Media Manager (Lead Gen-Meta, TikTok, YouTube & Google)

Location: Remote (Philippines)

Employment Type: Full Time

Salary: $1,500-$2000 per month

Experience Required: Minimum 5 Years

Work Hours: 9-6pm UK Time

How to Apply: 

Fill the form: https: // forms. gle/ Es1tdVhVP8iM 3afx6 (remove the spaces)



Role Overview

We are a fast growing UK pay per lead marketing and scalable agency focused on high volume lead generation for clients in the legal claim sector. Our lead gen campaigns include but it is not limited to personal injury, financial claims, US Motor vehicle a
 & more.

We're looking for an experienced paid media manager who understands the pay per lead model at a strategic level and has experience managing campaigns that generate thousands of leads every month while building the internal systems required to support consistency and guidance for junior media buyers.



Requirements

• Minimum 5 years experience managing direct response lead generation in B2C vertical like Personal Injury

 • Proven experience managing profitable lead generation campaigns

 • Experience managing or leading a team of media buyers

 • Strong analytical and data interpretation skills

 • Experience managing campaigns across Meta,  TikTok, Google and X

 • Understanding of landing page optimisation and conversion rate optimisation

 • Experience with tracking tools and integrations such as Google Tag Manager and Zapier

 • Experience building systems or processes that improve media buying operations

 • Ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously in a fast paced environment



Key Responsibilities

Campaign Strategy and Planning

• Develop and execute scalable media buying strategies for lead generation campaigns

 • Plan campaign structures, audiences, creatives and funnels to maximise ROI

 • Identify opportunities to scale profitable campaigns across multiple traffic sources



Campaign Management

• Launch, monitor and optimise campaigns to meet target CPLs & CVRs across multiple advertising platforms

 • Manage campaign budgets and ensure efficient allocation of ad spend

 • Continuously test audiences, creatives and funnels to improve performance

 • Maintain consistent campaign performance across multiple accounts



Team Leadership

 • Manage and oversee a small team of media buyers

 • Provide guidance, training and performance feedback

 • Ensure campaigns are executed according to strategy and best practices

 • Maintain accountability for team performance and campaign outcomes



Systems and Operational Management

 • Develop and maintain systems that improve media buying efficiency and consistency

 • Create and manage SOPs for campaign setup, optimisation and reporting

 • Implement workflows that ensure campaign quality control and reduce errors

 • Improve processes to support campaign scaling and team productivity



Data Analysis, Research and Reporting

- Analyse campaign data to identify trends, opportunities and inefficiencies

- Conduct market and competitor research to inform campaign strategy

- Produce performance reports with actionable insights

- Use data to guide scaling decisions and budget allocation

- Comfortable with google sheets and other reporting tools



Advertising Platforms

•  Meta Ads

 • Google Ads

 • X 
) Ads

 • TikTok Ads



Creative Development

- Be comfortable writing direct response text copy & video scripts 

- Develop static ad creatives to improve campaign performance

- Work with designers to iterate and optimise creatives

title: Video Editor & AI Content Creator for Social Media Ads
description:
ABOUT US:

Lucky Pets is a fast-growing German D2C brand selling premium 

nutritional supplements for dogs. Developed with veterinarians, 

manufactured in Germany. Our main sales channel is Meta 


 & Instagram) and we are scaling aggressively.



THE ROLE:

We are looking for a performance video editor who lives and breathes 

Meta advertising. You understand how VSLs work, how to structure 

long-form paid social content, and how to turn a script into a 

video that sells. You work fast, you work independently, and you 

deliver multiple finished videos per day.



WHAT YOU WILL WORK ON:



? VSLs (Video Sales Letters) — 3 to 5 minutes long

   The core of our Meta strategy. You receive a script. You turn it 

   into a fully finished, conversion-optimized video — including 

   sourcing visuals, B-roll, AI-generated content, captions, pacing, 

   and sound design. 



? Short-For
ta Ads — 30 to 90 seconds

   Hook-first, fast-paced, direct response ads for
 and 

   Instagram Feed and Reels.



FORMAT:

9:
 vertical for
 & Instagram. Same assets but maybe with slightly changes also run on 

Pinterest, YouTube Shorts, and TikTok.



YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES:

You own the video from script to finished file. That means:

? Sourcing all visuals — B-roll, stock footage, or AI-generated content

? Creating custom AI video content tailored to our brand and audience

   (cute, emotional, brand-fit dog visuals — animated, comic-style, 

   or cinematic AI footage). We have had good experience with Kling 

   — but you use whatever tool gets the best result.

? Captions, pacing, music, sound design

? Hook optimization — if you see a better way to open the script, 

   speak up. We value that.



We provide: the script and UGC material when available for the specific product.

You provide: everything else.



HOW WE WORK:

We keep things simple and structured. There is a clear task list 

that you work through. You have everything you need to get started and 

we trust you to deliver. We love working with people who are 

self-driven and take pride in their output.



Especially in the beginning, we will have more frequent check-ins 

and feedback rounds together — until we have found our shared style, 

vision, and quality standard. Once we are aligned, things will flow 

naturally from there. 



REQUIREMENTS:

? Proven experience editing Meta ads — VSLs and short-form. 

   Portfolio with real ad examples required.

? Deep understanding of VSL structure: hook ? problem ? agitation 

   ? solution ? proof ? offer ? CTA

? Knows how to stop the scroll in the first 2–3 seconds

? Strong self-sourcing skills — you find your own visuals

? AI video generation is a core skill, not optional. You must be 

   able to create high-quality AI visuals independently.

? Proficient in CapCut, Premiere Pro, or DaVinci Resolve

? Works fast, delivers in volume, manages own time



HIRING PROCESS:

Paid test project to start.

? You receive: company briefing and script

? Your task: deliver one finished Meta VSL (4–5 minutes)

? Turnaround: 3–4 days

? Pay: $20



If we love the result, we would love to bring you on full-time (40h/week) .

title: Finance Manager (Xero & Australian Compliance)
description:
Remote (Philippines) | Full-Time | Australian Business Hours



About Us



The Lucy Rose Clinic is an established Australian telehealth clinic specialising in women’s hormonal and thyroid health. We operate nationally and are currently strengthening our financial operations to support growth, stability, and improved cash flow visibility.



About the Role



We are seeking a highly capable Finance Manager with strong Xero and Australian accounting experience to take ownership of our financial operations.



This is a hands-on role responsible for both bookkeeping and financial oversight. You will replace the current bookkeeping function and take full ownership of finance operations over time.



The role will begin with an intensive onboarding period focused on reviewing, cleaning up, and stabilising financial systems. Ongoing hours and structure will be determined based on efficiency and business needs.



This is not a training role — we require someone who can step in, assess quickly, and take control.



Key Responsibilities



Xero Management & Clean-Up

Review, clean, and reconcile all accounts in Xero

Identify and fix errors, inconsistencies, and gaps

Maintain accurate, up-to-date financial records

BAS / IAS / GST Preparation

Prepare BAS and IAS for accountant review and lodgement

Ensure correct GST coding and compliance

Maintain ATO-ready financial records

Payroll & Super

Manage payroll processing and reconciliation

Ensure correct superannuation calculations and tracking

Support STP processes

Financial Reporting

Prepare weekly and monthly reports:

Profit & Loss

Cash Flow

Balance Sheet

Provide clear summaries and insights to support decision-making



Cash Flow & Financial Oversight



Monitor cash position and upcoming liabilities

Flag risks early and provide practical recommendations

Bookkeeping Ownership

Take over and manage the ongoing bookkeeping function

Ensure accuracy, consistency, and efficiency

Systems & Process Improvement

Implement structured month-end processes

Improve financial systems and workflows

Work closely with external accountant



Key Requirements (Essential)



Strong, hands-on experience using Xero (daily use)

Proven experience working with Australian clients



Experience preparing BAS / IAS (even if not lodging)

Strong understanding of GST and Australian compliance

Experience managing payroll and superannuation

Ability to work independently and take ownership

High attention to detail and accuracy

Clear, confident English communication



Reliability & Ownership



Demonstrated reliability in a full-time remote role

Consistently available and responsive during Australian business hours

Takes ownership of tasks and follows through without reminders



Highly Regarded



Experience cleaning up disorganised or messy books

Experience in service-based or healthcare businesses

Experience working directly with business owners



Key KPIs



Xero fully reconciled and accurate

BAS/IAS prepared correctly and on time

Weekly financial reporting delivered consistently

Clear visibility of cash flow and liabilities

Issues identified and resolved proactively



What Success Looks Like



Within 2 weeks: clear understanding of financial position

Within 4–6 weeks: books are clean, structured, and reliable

Ongoing: finance function runs smoothly with minimal oversight



What We Offer



Stable, long-term role

Direct access to business owner

Opportunity to take ownership of finance function

Support from external Australian accountant



How to Apply



Please include:



Your CV

A short video (2–3 minutes) answering:

Your experience with Xero and Australian BAS/GST

A time you cleaned up or fixed a company’s books

Your experience working with Australian clients

Confirmation that you are available to work consistently during Australian business hours



Applications without a video will not be considered.

title: Appointment Setter (Remote)
description:
We’re looking for someone who can help us convert inbound leads into scheduled appointments for our physical therapy clinic.



This is not a typical “read a script and book a call” type of role.



The people you’ll be speaking with are often dealing with real health issues — things like Parkinson’s, stroke recovery, or chronic pain. They’re not always ready to commit right away. Some are hesitant, some are skeptical, and a lot of them have already tried other treatments that didn’t work.



Your job is to talk to them like a real person, understand what’s going on, and help them feel comfortable taking the next step.



A big part of this role is being able to handle objections without making it awkward. People will say things like “I didn’t sign up for this,” or “I need to think about it,” or “I’m busy.” You can’t just back off when that happens — you need to be able to stay in the conversation, ask good questions, and guide them toward a decision.



We’re not looking for someone pushy. But we are looking for someone confident.



You’ll be making a lot of calls every week — usually around 200 or more — so consistency matters. Some days will be better than others, but the people who do well here don’t get discouraged easily. They keep showing up, keep dialing, and keep improving.



We also expect you to listen back to your own calls and get better over time. This isn’t a “set it and forget it” job. The more you improve your conversations, the better your results will be.



If you’ve done appointment setting or sales before, that’s great. If not, what matters more is how you communicate. Your English should feel natural, not robotic. You should be comfortable talking to people, asking questions, and keeping a conversation going.



This role is a good fit if you actually enjoy helping people make decisions and don’t shy away from uncomfortable conversations.



It’s probably not a good fit if you rely heavily on scripts or get thrown off when someone pushes back.



If this sounds like something you’d be good at, apply and include a short video introducing yourself and any call experience you have.



APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS (MANDATORY)



1. Record a CUSTOM video (1–2 minutes)



This must be:



Recent (recorded specifically for THIS job)



Not reused from other applications



In your video, answer:



Why you want this role specifically (not just “any job”)



What makes you confident you can succeed in a high-call, high-performance role



Your experience with handling objections or difficult conversations

If your video feels generic, templated, or reused — you will not be considered.



2. Show us how you think 



Record a short response (audio or video):



Objection:

"I’m not interested right now."



We’re NOT looking for a perfect script.



We’re looking for:



Confidence



Emotional intelligence



Ability to continue the conversation naturally



WHO WE ACTUALLY HIRE



We hire people who:



Take pride in their work



Want to get really good at sales and communication



Are comfortable having uncomfortable conversations



Don’t rely on scripts — they think and adapt in real time



Want long-term growth, not just a quick paycheck



WHO WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED



Mass applicants sending the same video everywhere



People avoiding the video requirement



Low-effort, rushed applications



People who don’t follow instructions exactly



FINAL NOTE



We respect people who:



Take the time to do things right



Put real effort into their application



Actually want this opportunity



If that’s you — we’re excited to see your application.

title: Long-Term Full-Time YouTube Video Editor (Adobe Premiere Pro + After Effects)
description:
I’m hiring a long-term video editor for my YouTube channel. The work is mainly clean, clarity-focused editing for game guides and commentary videos, using Adobe Premiere Pro and After Effects. Most edits are not overly complex, but they do require good motion sense, polished visual presentation, and consistency. This is planned as a full-time position, though project start timing can vary depending on content schedule and game updates. Most deadlines will be reasonable, but there will be rare fast-turnaround situations where I need a video edited quickly, especially around things like livestreams, breaking updates, or time-sensitive content.



Channel: SteamedBunX Reverse: 1999



I’m looking for a long-term editor to work with on my YouTube channel, focused primarily on gacha game content and guide-style videos.



The editing style is relatively light and clarity-focused. This is not a role centered around heavy cinematic editing or constant high-end VFX. The main goal is to create clean, effective visual aids that support what I’m explaining and help viewers follow the information more easily.



What the work mainly involves



Animating transitions between slide-like sections



Bringing on-screen elements into focus as they are discussed



Using basic but polished motion such as scale, position, opacity, and occasional masking



Working with B-roll, overlays, text callouts, cards, and other visual aids



Keeping the final result clean, readable, and consistent across videos



Software requirements



Strong Adobe Premiere Pro skills



Working proficiency in Adobe After Effects



Ability to comfortably use both depending on the needs of the project



Position structure / workload

This is currently planned as a full-time position. That said, the actual timing of when a project begins can vary depending on patch cycles, update schedules, livestreams, and content priorities.



Expected output is roughly 4–6 videos per month, though some periods may be lighter or heavier depending on the game itself and the content calendar.



Most projects will follow a normal workflow, but there will be rare occasions where I need a very fast turnaround, especially for time-sensitive uploads such as livestream-related coverage or update-driven videos that need to go out quickly.



Types of videos

Not every video requires the same amount of editing.



Character guides tend to be more visually involved:





Tier list or discussion-style videos are usually more static and rely more on B-roll:





There will also be simpler, fast-turnaround videos that are more time-sensitive:





These typically require less visual complexity, but they need to be completed quickly when timing matters.



What I will provide

For each video, I will provide a fully completed voice recording along with any necessary footage already placed on a Premiere Pro timeline. I will also include clear instructions for each section outlining what should appear on screen.



Depending on workflow preference, I can provide:



A full script with all image/video assets



A detailed chapter-by-chapter visual guide



Existing templates, text styles, overlays, card effects, and sample project files for consistency



All assets will be clearly labeled so the workflow is easy to follow.



What I’m looking for



Someone reliable and consistent for long-term collaboration



Someone who can work within an established style and keep things visually clean



Someone comfortable handling both standard guide videos and occasional urgent uploads



Someone who communicates well and can adapt to different project pacing when needed



Paid test project

I’d like to begin with a paid test run. I will provide a script from an upcoming video, and you will be compensated regardless of the outcome. Payment will be half upfront and half upon satisfactory completion.



I’d also prefer a quick 1-on-1 discussion before moving forward so we can align on workflow, communication, and expectations.



Turnaround time is open for discussion, but consistency is important so I can plan recording and uploads properly.

title: Outbound Calls - Sales Rep B2B SDR BDR
description:

We're seeking a Sales Development Representative to join our team.



As a Sales Development Representative, you will be responsible for outreach, prospecting, and lead qualification. Your key role will be to create and implement outbound sales strategies, make effective cold calls, prepare target lists, and nurture them to convert into an appointment with one of our inside sales rep.



The primary focus of this role is qualifying leads, from prospecting through appointment setting.



Key Responsibilities:

- Conduct cold and warm outreach to pre-qualify and schedule introductory meetings for the sales team.

- Identify, research, and qualify new prospects using Seamless.ai, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, and outbound outreach (calls and
).

- Track all prospect interactions, outreach, and follow-ups in HubSpot CRM.

- Collaborate with the CEO to refine lead generation strategies.



Qualifications:

- Minimum of 4 years of sales and cold calling.

- Proficiency in cold calling techniques.

- Fluent in English.

- Flawless communication skills, both written and oral.

- Exceptional ability to craft compelling follow-up
s that drive meeting bookings.

- Demonstrated ability to work solo as well as being a productive tea
mber, making outbound calls every day.

- Proficiency in HubSpot CRM, Seamless.ai, and LinkedIn Sales Navigator.

- Must be an excellent problem-solver and willing to collaborate in an exceedingly competitive environment.

- Work US business hours.



To Apply:

- Provide video introducing yourself answering these 3 questions:

1. What do you see as your 3 strongest attributes?

2. Why are you suited for this position? (provide what has been your best cold call opener)

3. Role play the opening of a cold call to a corporate prospect and show how you would move toward booking an introductory meeting. Their objection is: “I’m busy right now. Just send me an
.” Show how you would respond and continue the conversation!

Company Overview:

We are a full-service marketing firm that serves corporate clients that need additional marketing bandwidth and experienced support. We act as an extension of internal teams, helping companies execute and scale their marketing efforts when time, resources, or specialized expertise are limited. With more than 15 years in the industry, we are proud to be a women and minority-owned business focused on helping companies grow through smart, results-driven marketing.



If you're driven, dedicated to personal growth, and thrive on building relationships with clients we encourage you to apply for this fulfilling opportunity.

title: Senior SEO Specialist
description:
Job Title: Senior SEO Specialist

Location: Remote / Hybrid (Flexible)

Type: Full-Time



About the Role

We are looking for a Senior SEO Specialist who can drive meaningful organic growth, not just rankings. You will take ownership of SEO strategy and execution across key areas, including on-page SEO, technical SEO, internal linking, and content optimisation.



This role suits someone with either broad SEO expertise or deep specialisation in a particular area of SEO, with the ability to deliver measurable results.



What You’ll Be Doing

- Plan and execute SEO strategies that drive traffic, leads, and revenue

- Optimise websites using on-page SEO best practices (content, metadata, structure)

- Identify and implement technical SEO improvements (site speed, indexing, crawlability, schema, etc.)

- Develop and manage internal linking strategies to improve site architecture and authority flow

- Conduct keyword research and content gap analysis

- Monitor performance using tools such as Google Search Console, GA4, Ahrefs, SEMrush, etc.

- Collaborate with content, development, and marketing teams to execute SEO initiatives

- Stay up to date with algorithm changes and industry trends



What We’re Looking For

- 4+ years of experience in SEO

- Strong understanding of on-page SEO, technical SEO, and site structure

- Proven track record of improving organic traffic and rankings

- Experience with SEO tools and analytics platforms

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

- Ability to think strategically and execute effectively



Bonus Points

- Experience working on large-scale websites or within an agency environment

- Technical background (HTML, CSS, JavaScript SEO)

- Experience with content strategy or CRO



Application Requirements

1. Please include the following in your application:

- What area of SEO do you consider your strongest, and why?

(e.g. technical SEO, on-page/content, link building, internal linking, etc.)

2. Describe three specific SEO initiatives you have worked on.

For each, explain:

- What you did

- Why you did it

- How you measured and confirmed that it improved SEO performance



Why Join Us?

- Opportunity to work on high-impact SEO projects

- Flexible working environment

- Ability to influence and shape SEO strategy

- Apply now if you are confident in your ability to deliver measurable SEO outcomes.



Thank you :)

title: Operations Manager for eCommerce Agency
description:
We are a growing eCommerce agency working with multiple international clients, and we are looking for a sharp, highly organized, and high-energy Operations Manager who can also handle project management at a strong level.



This is a very important role inside the business.



You will be the person responsible for helping keep the agency organized, making sure tasks are clearly delegated, deadlines are met, communication is strong, and team output is high quality. This is not a passive role and not a basic admin role. We need someone who can think fast, move fast, lead properly, and keep the team aligned.



This role is best for someone who has real experience in operations, team management, project management, eCommerce, and client communication.



What You’ll Be Responsible For

Operations and Team Management



-Manage the day-to-day operations of the agency

-Oversee remote tea
mbers and make sure everyone is aligned and productive

-Delegate tasks clearly and properly based on priority and skill level

-Follow up with tea
mbers to make sure tasks are moving and deadlines are being met

-Spot bottlenecks, delays, and weak performance early and address them fast

-Help improve accountability, structure, and overall team discipline



Project Management



-Take client requests, meeting notes, Loom videos, and feedback and turn them into clear, actionable tasks

-Assign tasks inside ClickUp or other project management tools with proper context, deadlines, and priorities

-Make sure tasks are not just posted, but actually understood by the team

-Keep projects moving forward without needing constant follow-up from the owner

-Ensure urgent tasks are handled on time and important items do not get delayed



Quality Control and Delivery



-Review work before it goes back to the client

-Make sure deliverables are accurate, polished, and aligned with what the client asked for

-Catch mistakes, weak output, or missing details before submission

-Maintain strong internal quality control across all active client projects



Client Communication



-Help manage client communication in a professional, clear, and confident way

-Send updates, follow-ups, and replies on time

-Make sure clients feel taken care of, updated, and supported

-Understand the client’s priorities and communicate them clearly to the team



Systems and Process Improvement



-Improve internal workflows and agency systems

-Help build better SOPs, structure, and processes

-Suggest ways to make the business more efficient

-Identify areas where automation or better systems can save time and improve delivery



What We’re Looking For



-Proven experience as an Operations Manager, Project Manager, or similar leadership role

-Strong experience in eCommerce, websites, and digital marketing environments

-Excellent written and verbal English communication

-Strong leadership and delegation skills

-Very organized, detail-oriented, and proactive

-Able to work with urgency and manage multiple moving parts

-Strong problem-solving ability

-Comfortable managing remote teams

-Comfortable using tools like ClickUp, Slack, Google Workspace, Loom, and similar platforms

-Able to take ownership and reduce the owner’s workload, not add to it



Big Plus If You Have Experience With



-Shopify

-WordPress

-Klaviyo

-Customer support workflows

-Order processing

-Inventory updates

-Canva or basic design coordination

-CRM and automation tools

-Agency operations

-Managing multiple client accounts at once



Important for This Role



This is a full-time, high-focus role inside a small but growing agency.



Because this position is crucial to daily operations, we are looking for someone who can give this role their full attention and energy. You must not have other jobs or active side commitments that will affect your focus, availability, or performance here.



We need someone fully committed, dependable, responsive, and ready to take real ownership.



Who This Role Is For



This role is for someone who:



-likes structure

-can lead people properly

-knows how to manage pressure

-can keep projects organized

-communicates clearly

-has strong urgency

-takes pride in keeping the business running smoothly



This is not for someone who only wants to post tasks, wait for instructions, or do the bare minimum.



Position Details



-Full-time

-Long-term

-Remote

-High ownership role

-Growth opportunity for the right person



How to Apply



To show that you actually read this job post, include the word juice in your cover letter.



Please send:



-Your resume

-A short cover letter explaining your relevant experience

-Why you are a strong fit for this role

-The tools and platforms you have worked with

-Your previous experience managing teams, projects, or agency operations

-If you have strong operations and project management experience in an eCommerce or digital agency setting, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Full-Time Vacation Planner Needed ($7/ HR + Insurance + Time-off)
description:
Deal Hospitality is currently seeking an organized, detail-oriented Vacation Planner to join our team. This position wears many hats including:



> Concierge/ Guest Services – pivotal to our success and sustainability, it goes without saying we need to be highly attentive to our guests needs, local knowledge, anticipating needs, working with ops teams for special occasions

> Reservations – extensive property knowledge, creating & adding to guest profiles, keeping response times low and conversion rates high

> Office Admin – lots of miscellaneous tasks such as data entry, auditing our listings, writing photo captions, sending reports, and assisting in our month end process



This is a pivotal role within our growing organization and therefore we are only seeking passionate, hardworking rock stars to apply.



About Deal Hospitality

Deal Hospitality is a boutique hospitality firm catering to a small group of fast growth companies specializing in larger high-quality short-term vacation homes. Formed in 2018 with an emphasis on creative & unique solutions.



Some Of What You’ll Be Doing

• Responding to guests inquiries,
s and messages with an emphasis on sense of urgency

• Keeping our property management system true & clean at all times by processing reservations, inquiries, owner stay requests, etc.

• Processing guest payments, sending payment requests, and late reminders

• Answer incoming phone lines

• Coordinate with appropriate vendors and personnel for all VIP, owner, and special request reservations to ensure they are 110% ready for arrival, no excuses

• Effectively communicating with the operations teams

• Auditing our public listings for accuracy

• Proper reporting/ logging of all pertinent information

• Process & distribute arrival reports daily

• Accurate vendor scheduling to ensure nothing is missed

• Documenting our processes and checklists, building our SOP manual dubbed “The Play Book”

• Additional projects as requested



What You Need

• Quiet dedicated workspace w/ stable, secure high-speed internet connection w/ backup internet option

• Out of the box thinker & aggressive problem solver – you don’t let anything stand in your way – no excuses

• Detail-oriented would be an understatement

• Excellent time management, communication & organizational skills – we have a deep appreciation for genuine hospitality which means ensuring no balls get dropped

• Exceptional social & people skills – you’ll need to be able to make sincere connections with guests, potential guests, community members, owners and vendors

• Team player – this is a startup, we all wear many hats

• Tech-savvy/ quick learner since we utilize various computer systems

• Vacation Rental/ Hotel/ hospitality experience (preferred)

• Flexible schedule as our busy times fluctuate



What We Offer

• Competitive Salary - 16,685 PHP // $280 USD per week

• Generous Vacation Policy - 13 paid days + 5 unpaid days if needed, 18 paid days after 3 years

• Health Insurance Stipend of 2,385 PHP // $40 USD per month

• YETI (Year End Team Incentive) Program

• Apple Workstation

• Annual Holiday Gathering

• Growth Opportunities



IF INTERESTED:

• Please submit your resume + brief video introduction of yourself

• You will NOT be considered until both of these items are submitted

• Submit your information at OnlineJobs.ph

•
 and LinkedIn submissions will not be reviewed

• We will reach out to you directly if we’d like to move forward – PLEASE DO NOT CALL

title: Social Media Content Operator
description:
We do not need someone to manage our brand, we need someone to execute it. Consistently, Cleanly, and without hand holding. If you've edited for a real estate agent, lifestyle brand, or real estate niche before, you already speak our language. Our content drives inbound leads every month. We're looking for a content operator to take raw videos and turn them into edited polished, platform ready content across instagram, tikTok, and YouTube shorts. This is a part time remote role with consistent weekly work. You'll receive footage and brand assets. Your job is to edit , format, waterfall, and post. You'll also oversee instagram DM's, automations, Newsletter and our CRM. 



What you'll do: 



- Edit raw video footage into short form content (Reels, TikToks, Shorts) 



- Add music, captions following brand guidelines 



- Repurpose videos, & long form text content across all platforms (waterfall approach, 1 long form into multiple short form pieces of content) 



- Maintain a simple content calendar to track output and posting schedule. 



What we're looking for: 



- Editing experience in basic apps like caplet or instagram edits 



- Experience in Real Estate content is not required but preferred



- Strong attention to detail 



- Reliable, responsive, able to meet turnaround times 



- Comfortable with Canva/Google Drive / Notion for file handoff and basic scheduling tools



What this is not: 



- Full time job, while it will be available in the future as of now we only need 2-3 active days expecting 10 hrs per week. 



- A creative director position - brand direction is already set, you just execute it. 



- A strategy Role- You post what's needed and given, on schedule that's provided 



If you'd like to send samples of work in the past that would be excellent. Thank you.

title: Video Editor (Short Form & Long Form)
description:
***NICHE/INDUSTRY***

Airbnb/Real Estate



We are mainly looking for a video editor to help edit short-form content for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts, as well as long-form educational videos for YouTube. This is ongoing weekly work, not a one-time project, and starts part-time with the potential to grow.



This is educational, operator-style content. Clean, credible, and direct. Not high-energy entertainment



Your main responsibility will be video editing.

- Edit short-form videos for Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts

- Edit long-form educational YouTube videos (talking-head, clean pacing)

- Add captions or subtitles

- Add b-roll, simple zooms, transitions, light motion graphics, and sound design

- Maintain a clean and consistent editing style



In addition to video editing, if you have other skills or are open to learning, you may also help with extra content tasks. These are a plus, not required.

- Create Instagram carousel posts using scripts provided

- Design simple
-style carousel posts that are easy to read on mobile

- Use Canva (preferred) or similar tools for carousel creation

- Help with basic AI-assisted visuals (cartoon or illustrated style) if needed

- AI tools, access, and guidance can be provided



Required skills for this role.

- Strong video editing skills, especially short-form content

- Comfortable editing long-form YouTube videos or willing to learn

- Experience with captions, b-roll, and social-media-style edits

- Reliable communication and consistent turnaround times

- Strong attention to detail



Nice to have but not required.

- Experience with Canva or simple graphic design

- Familiarity with Instagram carousel posts

- Interest in AI tools for content creation

- Understanding of hooks, pacing, and storytelling

- Interest in long-term collaboration



Work structure and pay.

- Ongoing weekly work

- Starting part-time with the potential to increase hours

- Pay depends on editing quality, speed, reliability, and additional skills



To apply, please include.

- Portfolio must include examples of educational or talking-head content you've edited. YouTube videos, course content, or similar. If your portfolio is mostly food, gaming, vlogs, or entertainment content, this likely isn't the right fit

- Links to Instagram accounts and Youtube accounts you worked with

- Any carousel or design work if available

- Your weekly availability

- Your rate expectations



To confirm you read this fully, include the phrase “Content > Comfort” somewhere in your application.

This role is primarily for a video editor. Extra skills like Canva, carousel creation, or AI visuals are a bonus and can lead to more work over time.

title: Global Sourcing Specialist-Assistant
description:
Job Title: Global Sourcing Specialist - Assistant



Company: Picnic Time, Inc. DBA Picnic Time Family of Brands



Picnic Time is a successful & sunny SoCal-based wholesale supplier of premium outdoor leisure, gift, and promotional products established in a garage in 1982. (Don’t worry, we have a real office now.) We know picnic, we love picnic, and we live picnic – it’s literally in our name.

Who we are: The Picnic Time Family is all about the people. We're a diverse team of hard-working yet easy-going people that care about what we're doing. If it weren't for the amazing group of people at Picnic Time, we wouldn't be where we are today.

What we do: We create quality products that are thoughtful, sustainable, and fun.  

When we do it:  We've been innovating since 1982 and we don't have any intention of slowing down.  The hope is that we'll be here creating great stuff for your kids' kids' kids!

Where the magic happens:  We were born in a small garage in West Hills, CA and moved to a bigger (ware)house in a small town, Moorpark, CA, in 1990.  We've been rockin’ the suburbs ever since!

Why we do what we do (this is the big one):  Our purpose for existing is to bring family and friends together so that they can make lasting memories...because that's what happiness is all about.  Each and every decision we make is ultimately geared toward that goal.

How we do it:  We're constantly driving towards our purpose via our 8 core values of Fun, Growth, Integrity, Collaboration, Innovation, Service, Productivity, and Passion.



Mission Statement: To create products that inspire friends and family to come together and make lasting memories – and have a great time doing it.



Job Description: Under the direction of our Global Sourcing Specialist, you will work to source products from around the world that meet the design, quality and pricing needs of our retail customers for private label and special direct import programs.



Role Overview

We are looking for a highly organized and detail-oriented Global Sourcing Specialist to manage our international supply chain and product development cycle. In this role, you will be the primary bridge between our design/sales teams and our global manufacturing partners (with a heavy focus on our partner factories in China). You will be responsible for obtaining competitive quotes, managing sample development, and ensuring that our production timelines and quality standards are met for every project.

________________________________________

Key Responsibilities

•	Factory Liaison: Act as the main point of contact for international factories, managing daily communication regarding production status, material availability, and lead times.

•	RFQs & Quotations: Manage the Request for Quote (RFQ) process; analyze factory quotes to ensure accuracy and competitive pricing, while maintaining target profit margins, and calculate landed costs for customer price quotes. Extensive use of MS Excel and Google Sheets.

•	Product Development: Oversee the sample lifecycle from initial concept and swatches to final pre-production approval. 

•	Tech Packs: Collect all product data, BOM's, and all item specific information needed to complete Tech Packet in product development software.

•	Quality & Compliance: Ensure all products meet client specifications and regulatory standards. Review third party inspection reports for compliance.

•	Strategic Sourcing: Works within the Global Sourcing team to develop strategic item assortments for seasonal buys and specific buyers. Study market trends and do competitive analysis and research

•	Digital Library (Salsify): Maintain a digital sample library of potential products in a Product Database (Salsify). Collect new catalogs from factories and all new items and input them into Salsify.

•	Sales Presentations: Must be comfortable creating presentations in PowerPoint, using snipping tool, and Excel.

•	Interdepartmental Communication: Must be comfortable communicating with all departments including Sales, Licensing, Product Development, Logistics, and Promotional teams. 

________________________________________

Qualifications & Skills

•	Experience: 3–5 years in global sourcing, procurement, or product development (consumer goods preferred). A good eye for products that are on trend and fit customers’ needs. Applicant must be proficient in Excel, Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator.

•	Communication & Organization: 

o	Communication: The ability to manage change and prioritize tasks with frequent interruptions are vital to this position, as well as being able to determine the best way to catalog and communicate information in writing (documents/emails) and visual aids (photos/illustrations) to ensure product consistency from one order to the next order

o	Organizational Excellence: Applicants must be organized with good time-management skills, be self-directed yet be able to take direction and ask questions, be good problem solvers with critical thinking skills, focused on effectiveness, efficiency, and must possess competent written and verbal communication skills. 

•	Technical Proficiency: Strong Excel skills (v-lookups, pivot tables) and experience with ERP/PLM software. 

•	Analytical Mindset: Ability to spot pricing discrepancies and analyze cost breakdowns (e.g., nesting vs. individual packaging).

•	Attention to Detail: Meticulous in tracking multiple projects simultaneously without losing sight of small specifications like Pantone colors or material finishes.

________________________________________

Bonus Points If You Have:

•	Experience with specific materials (e.g., textiles, wood, metals).

•	Prior experience in the promotional products or consumer goods industry.





Reports to: Global Sourcing Specialist, Supply Chain Department



Location: Remote – VPN access to virtual desk in Moorpark, CA 93021, USA

Hours: 8 AM –4:30 PM Monday – Friday PST (West Coast USA)

Salary: US$5 per hour (Paid $400 every two weeks) 

Employee Type: Contract 40 hours per week

title: $1,500/Month Klaviyo Manager For Growing US Email Agency
description:
We’re hiring ONE person to run operations while the founder focuses on strategy and growth. You’d be the first hire — not employee #47, employee #1.



The person who proves themselves here becomes Head of Operations as we scale. Real title, real team under you, real money. If you want to build something, keep reading.



Why This Role Is Worth Your Attention



 • Ground floor: First hire. You’ll have seniority over everyone who comes after you.

 • Clear raises: $900–$1,300 ? $1,400–$1,800 by Month 7 ? $2,500+/mo in Year 2. Tied to metrics, not promises.

 • Real career path: VA ? Account Manager ? Head of Ops. You’ll manage a team as we grow.

 • Direct founder mentorship on
 strategy, e-commerce, and agency operations.

 • Stability: Paid on time every time. 10 days PTO after 6 months. Annual raises.



What You’ll Own

 • Klaviyo — upload campaigns, build flows, QA everything before it goes live

 • Quality control — review every
 design before clients see it

 • Project management — track 50–100+
s across multiple brands, keep designers on deadline

 • Client communication — daily Slack updates with US/UK brands

 • Figma reviews — verify all revisions are applied, comments resolved



Requirements (Non-Negotiable)



 • 1+ year hands-on Klaviyo (campaigns, flows, segments — we will screen-share verify)

 • Worked with 5+ e-commerce/DTC brands (not SaaS, not B2B)

 • Fluent English — written AND spoken (you’ll message clients directly)

 • Available 9 AM–5 PM EST (min 6 hours overlap)

 • Figma + PM tool experience (Notion, ClickUp, Asana, or similar)



Don’t Apply If:



 • You’ve never actually used Klaviyo. “I’m a fast learner” and “I’ve seen it” don’t count. You must have logged in, built campaigns, and set up flows yourself.

 • Your background is not e-commerce/DTC. SaaS, B2B, newsletters — different world. We need someone who knows Shopify brands, product launches, and revenue-driven
.

 • Your English needs heavy editing to sound professional. Our clients are US/UK brands paying premium prices. Every message you send represents us. It needs to sound natural, not translated.

 • This would be a side gig. If you’re juggling 2–3 other clients, you won’t have the bandwidth. This is a full 40-hour commitment.

 • You need to be micromanaged. We’ll train you, but after that you need to own your domain and execute without being chased every hour.

 • You’re mass-applying to 50 jobs right now. We can tell. If your application doesn’t reference IX Media or this specific role, it goes straight in the trash.

 • You’re using AI to write your application. We will know. Instant reject.



How to Apply



Hiring ONE person. Missing any item = deleted.



 • Loom Video (60 sec): (1) Why you’re right for this role. (2) A time you caught a mistake before it reached a client. (3) What “owning your role” means to you.



This is a real opportunity to build something. If you take pride in catching every detail and want a career, not a gig — apply now. Reviewing this week.

title: B2B Appointment Setter ($200/closed deal + $25/Show-up + Bonuses)
description:
* PLEASE READ THE ENTIRE DESCRIPTION BEFORE APPLYING *



IMPORTANT: We are only looking for someone with specifically B2B cold calling experience. If you have only worked inbound leads, warm leads, or leads from an ad, please do not apply.



---------- ---------- ---------- ---------- ----------



Hi — my name is Jordan, and I’m the founder of a company that helps small businesses never miss a call using an A.I. Receptionist. We help service businesses capture missed calls and turn them into real customers instead of losing them to competitors.



We are not a large corporate call center where employees are treated like numbers. Right now I am looking to add only 1–2 experienced B2B cold callers who want a long-term role with the opportunity to make good money and grow with our business.



If you have experience doing cold outreach to small businesses and have the skills to schedule an appointment, this is the job for you!



WHAT YOU WILL DO:

• Source B2B leads (leads are not provided)

• Make outbound cold calls

• Introduce our service in a clear and simple way

• Book qualified prospects for a free consultation

• Confirm that the appointments you book actually show up

• You do not close the deals, our team handles the sales calls. Your job is to start conversations and book quality appointments.



THIS ROLE IS A GOOD FIT IF YOU:

• Have real B2B cold calling experience

• Speak clear, confident English

• Have reliable internet and a quiet workspace

• Are comfortable making high-volume outbound calls

• Are consistent, disciplined, and reliable



COMPENSATION:



Training Period (14 Days)

• $5/hour base

 $25 for every attended appointment (show-up)

• $100 for every deal that closes



After Training Period

• $25 for every attended appointment (show-up) 

• $200 per closed 

• Huge bonuses ($50-$500)



Example Months (For Part Time Role):

8 Show-ups ($200) + 2 Sold ($400) + Bonus ($50) = $650

12 Show-ups ($300) + 3 Sold ($600) + Bonus ($200) = $1,100

16 Show-ups ($400) + 4 Sold ($800) + Bonus ($300) = $1,500



HOURS:

• Part-time (20-30 hours per week) to start with the option to transition to full time

• Schedule: 1:00 AM – 7:00 AM Philippine Time



HOW TO APPLY:

• Please do not send a generic message or one written with chat GPT - those will be ignored.

• Do not mention previous jobs that are not specifically related to cold calling B2B

• Include the following 4 things in your application:

-   1) How many months/years of experience do you have specifically cold calling B2B

-   2) Describe in detail your experience: (Exactly what service/product were you offering and to who)

-   3) How successful were you in booking appointments?

-   4) Put "I am a hustler" in the subject line.

title: Remote SaaS Sales Rep (Part-Time, Results-Driven, Growth Opportunity)
description:
We are looking for a part-time sales professional to help sell our SaaS product, PageSentinel.



PageSentinel helps businesses monitor websites for important changes, unexpected edits, and potential issues. We are looking for someone who knows how to sell, can work independently, and is focused on getting real results.



This is not a role for someone who needs constant direction. I am looking for someone who understands online selling, can figure out how to generate leads and close deals with a limited ad budget, and can help grow this product.



My goal is simple: I want someone who can sell.



I enjoy building SaaS products, not selling them. I plan to keep launching new SaaS products over time, so this role has real growth potential for the right person. If you perform well and help drive sales, I would be happy to grow this into a full-time position with more opportunity.



**What you’ll be doing**



* Sell PageSentinel to businesses that need website monitoring

* Find and reach out to potential customers online

* Develop and test ways to get leads and close sales with a limited budget

* Handle follow-up conversations and move prospects toward purchase

* Help improve messaging and positioning based on what you learn from prospects

* Focus on results, not busywork



**What I’m looking for**



* Proven sales experience, ideally with SaaS, digital products, or online services

* Strong communication and persuasion skills

* Someone who is confident selling without needing a big ad budget

* Self-motivated and resourceful

* Comfortable working part-time to start, with the potential to grow into full-time

* Someone who cares about performance and can deliver results



**Important**

This role is for someone who can actually sell, not someone who just wants to “help with marketing.” I need someone who understands how to turn outreach, conversations, and opportunities into paying customers.



If you are good at sales and can prove it, there will be plenty of room to grow. I am building more SaaS products, so this could become a long-term opportunity for the right person.



**To apply**

Please send:



* A short introduction about yourself

* Your sales experience

* What kinds of products or services you have sold before

* Examples of results you have achieved

* How you would approach selling a SaaS product like PageSentinel with a limited budget

* Your required pay

title: Revenue Cycle Specialist
description:
At Premier Eye Associates, we don’t just see patients — we treat them like family. Our mission is to deliver world-class eye care with legendary service that feels like a five-star experience. We’re seeking a Revenue Cycle Specialist who brings that same excellence and warmth to our business operations.



About the Role



The Revenue Cycle employee oversees all aspects of medical and vision billing to ensure accurate, timely reimbursement and a seamless financial experience for our patients. This role blends technical precision with collaborative leadership — ideal for someone who thrives on organization, coaching others, and improving systems that support both staff and patient satisfaction.



Key Responsibilities



Oversee end-to-end billing and reimbursement for medical and vision claims.

Verify insurance eligibility, benefits, and authorizations.

Train and coach staff on accurate coding (CPT, ICD-10, modifiers).

Monitor denials, submit appeals, and resolve unpaid claims efficiently.

Maintain credentialing for providers and locations (CAQH, NPI, directories).

Generate and review patient statements and resolve billing inquiries.

Analyze reports to identify trends and improve revenue cycle efficiency.

Collaborate with leadership to optimize processes and support team growth.

What We’re Looking For



Strong understanding of medical billing and coding (optometry experience a plus).

Exceptional communication and problem-solving skills.

Detail-oriented, proactive, and comfortable leading a small team.

Proficient in EMR systems (RevolutionEHR preferred).

High level of professionalism and a commitment to exceptional service.

Why Join Premier Eye Associates



You’ll join a fast-growing, forward-thinking optometry group where innovation and patient care go hand in hand. We believe in empowering our team, celebrating wins, and continuously improving — both in how we care for patients and how we support one another.

s

s must exactly match Apollo

s
