title: Instagram Content & Creator Management VA (Manual Posting + Repurposing)
description:
We are looking for a highly organized and detail-oriented VA to help manage our Instagram content and coordinate with multiple creators.



This is an execution role, not a strategy role.



You will be responsible for ensuring all content is fully prepared, organized, and ready before posting across platforms.



This role supports a growing content and marketing team, so attention to detail, consistency, and reliability are critical.



We value people who take initiative and improve systems—not just follow instructions.



If you enjoy organizing content, working within clear systems, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks, this role is a great fit.





About Us



We are an online health and fitness education company focused on helping women improve pelvic health through exercise-based programs.



Our content is educational, practical, and designed to help women reduce symptoms like leaking, pelvic pressure, and discomfort—without relying on invasive treatments.





Responsibilities



- Content Posting (Primary)

- Manually post Instagram Reels and posts

- Format captions cleanly (spacing, readability, emojis if applicable)

- Add hashtags and tags

- Select covers/thumbnails

- Ensure content is posted correctly and on time

- Content Repurposing

- Take Instagram content and repost it to:

        - TikTok

        - YouTube Shorts

        -
 Reels

        - Adjust captions slightly per platform if needed

- Creator Coordination

- Manage content from ~5 creators (10+ posts per month)

- Track who has submitted content and who hasn’t

- Follow up on deadlines

- Organize all incoming content





Content Preparation (Light Editing)



- Add captions/subtitles to videos (using provided tools/software)

- Add simple text overlays or intro banners when needed

- Trim or adjust clips for platform requirements

- Prepare videos for posting across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts



Advanced or creative editing is handled by our editing team.





Content Organization



- Maintain a simple content tracker (Google Sheets or Notion)

- Track status: Not Ready / Ready / Posted

- Keep folders and files organized

- Ensure nothing gets missed





Communication



- Provide clear updates on what is posted and what is pending

- Ask questions when something is unclear

- Flag missing assets or issues early





Important Notes



You will NOT be responsible for strategy or content ideas

You will NOT be writing long-form copy from scratch

You WILL be responsible for execution, organization, and consistency





Requirements



- Experience posting Instagram Reels manually

- Familiar with TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube Shorts formatting

- Strong written English (for captions and communication)

- Highly organized and detail-oriented

- Reliable and responsive

- Able to follow systems and improve them over time





Bonus (Not Required)



- Experience managing multiple creators

- Experience with content tracking systems (Notion, Google Sheets)

- Understanding of short-form content formats





Trial Process



Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a paid 1-week trial.



During the trial, you will:



- Work with 5–10 pieces of content

- Prepare posts for Instagram and other platforms

- Organize content in a tracker

- Communicate progress clearly





How to Apply (IMPORTANT – read carefully)



To be considered, follow these steps exactly:



- Start your application title with the word: BUFF

- Share links to 2–3 Instagram accounts you have helped manage

- Record a short Loom video (2–3 minutes) explaining:

         - How you would organize content from multiple creators

         - How you would make sure nothing is missed



Applications that do not follow these steps will not be reviewed.





Compensation



Paid trial: $50–$100 (1 week)



This role will start part-time (20–30 hours/week) and grow into a larger/full-time position as content volume increases.

Monthly compensation will be discussed based on experience and adjusted as responsibilities grow.

title: Marketing Technical & Automation Specialist (GoHighLevel Expert)
description:
Company: Mill Creek Marketing

Location: Remote

Time Zone: 9am-5pm CST, Mon–Fri 

Hours: Full-time (30-40 hrs/wk)  

Pay: $8 - $10/hr depending on experience 



THE ROLE



Mill Creek Marketing is hiring a Marketing Technical & Automation Specialist to build, maintain, and improve the systems that power our clients’ marketing and sales pipelines.



This role is for someone who is deeply experienced in GoHighLevel and marketing tech infrastructure — not a beginner.



You will be responsible for the technical backbone of client delivery, ensuring that funnels, websites, tracking, automations, and integrations are built correctly and operating smoothly.



This is not a task-only role.



We are looking for someone who:



• Understands how marketing systems actually work

• Can take direction and run with projects independently

• Identifies opportunities to improve systems and client experience

• Builds clean, reliable infrastructure that scales



ABOUT MILL CREEK MARKETING



Mill Creek Marketing helps residential contractors grow their businesses without creating more chaos.



We specialize in building the marketing and sales systems contractors need to scale profitably — including lead generation, pipeline management, automation, and sales system infrastructure. 



Our clients rely on us not just for marketing, but for the systems behind the marketing.



OUR CORE VALUES (ROOTS)



Raise The Standard

Outwork Bad Days

Opportunity Mindset

Take Extreme Ownership

Simplify & Systematize



WHAT YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR



You will own the technical setup and optimization of marketing and sales systems for our clients and for us as an agency. 



This includes:



GoHighLevel System Builds



• Full CRM setup for new clients

• Pipeline builds and automation workflows

• Smart lists, triggers, custom values, and tagging structures

•
/SMS campaign deployment

• Lead routing and notification systems



Funnel & Website Development



• Building funnels inside GoHighLevel

• Building landing pages and websites in WordPress

• Form integrations and conversion optimization

• Ensuring mobile responsiveness and fast page load



Marketing Tracking Infrastructure



• Installing and verifying Meta Pixel events

• Setting up Google Tag Manager tracking

• Troubleshooting conversion tracking issues

• Implementing attribution tools such as Cometly or Hyros



Integrations & Automation



• Connecting tools via Zapier or Make

• Managing webhooks and API integrations

• CRM integrations and automation logic

• Troubleshooting broken automations



Systems Optimization



• Identifying broken or inefficient workflows

• Improving automation and pipeline structures

• Recommending system improvements

• Reducing operational friction for the team



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE IN THIS ROLE & ON THIS TEAM



Within the first 30-45 days you will:



• Launch new client GHL setups within 5–7 days of onboarding

• Deploy reactivation campaigns within 48–72 hours

• Ensure tracking and pixels are firing correctly across platforms

• Reduce automation errors and tech issues

• Improve internal systems and documentation



The best candidates will also proactively suggest improvements like:



• New automation opportunities

• Better tracking setups

• More efficient workflows

• Improvements to the client experience



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE



You should already be comfortable working independently inside the following systems:



GoHighLevel



• Workflow automation

• Pipeline setup

• Smart lists and tagging

• Custom values and triggers

• A2P setup and SMS/email automation



WordPress



• Landing page builds

• Funnel page creation

• Plugin configuration

• Form integrations



Marketing Tracking



• Meta Pixel setup and event verification

• Google Tag Manager

• Conversion tracking troubleshooting

• Attribution tools like Cometly or Hyros



Technical Skills



• Zapier or Make integrations

• DNS/domain troubleshooting

• Script embeds and event tracking

• Automation logic (if/then triggers)



THE KIND OF PERSON WHO THRIVES IN THIS ROLE



You may be a great fit if you:



• Are extremely detail-oriented and very thorough 

• Naturally think in systems and processes

• Enjoy solving technical problems

• Take ownership of your work without being chased or micromanaged 

• Are comfortable figuring things out independently when needed 



You don't wait to be told every little step and sub-step. You take the objective and direction given to you and execute it intelligently, asking for help when you've exhausted your other resources. 



COMMUNICATION EXPECTATIONS



We run a high-accountability team and rely on clear, proactive communication.



You will be expected to:



• Check Slack and ClickUp daily

• Communicate blockers or confusion early

• Update tasks and progress consistently in ClickUp 

• Respond promptly during main working hours (9am-5pm CST)



THIS ROLE IS DEFINITELY NOT A GOOD FIT FOR YOU IF:



• You need step-by-step instructions for everything 

• You frequently miss details, rush through work, or often have to correct or redo your work 

• You disappear for long periods without communication

• You are not comfortable troubleshooting technical issues

• You take feedback personally and are hurt by it, rather than as a chance to grow and get better 



THIS ROLE IS A GOOD FIT FOR YOU IF:



• You enjoy building marketing systems

• You like fixing broken tech and improving processes

• You are highly organized, thorough, and detail-oriented

• You want to grow with a fast-moving team



HOW TO APPLY



Send us a message with the subject line: "Ricky Bobby Tech Specialist"



Record us a personalized 3-5 minute Loom video where you:



- Introduce yourself and your background

- Explain your experience as a technical specialist working in GHL 

- Walk through 1-2 case studies (blur any sensitive info) where you built or improved a system or workflow 

- Show how you self-QA your work to avoid errors 

- Tell us why you believe you are the perfect fit for this specific role at Mill Creek Marketing 



Attach your resume, and any relevant screenshots, references, or portfolio links.

title: Estimator/Quantity Surveyor
description:
Job Overview:

We're seeking an experienced Construction Estimator/Quantity Surveyor to join our growing team. This role focuses on delivering high-quality estimation services across commercial, residential, and trade sectors. Working directly with the team and the CEO, you'll handle complex estimation projects while helping maintain our reputation for technical excellence and accuracy.



Key Responsibilities:

- Prepare detailed cost estimates and bills of quantities for construction projects

- Develop and maintain rate libraries and estimation templates

- Manage tender submissions and provide comprehensive documentation

- Analyse project specifications and drawings to ensure accurate quantity take-offs

- Provide value management and cost planning services

- Support strategic partnerships with key clients

- Mentor other tea
mbers in estimation practices



Required Technical Skills:

- Minimum 3-5 years experience in construction estimation or quantity surveying

- Experience in the Australian Construction Industry

- Proven track record of accurate estimation across various project types



Required Software Skills: 

- Advanced proficiency in Cubit or similar estimation software

- Expert level Excel skills for custom calculations and analysis

- Harvest 

- Airtable

- Microsoft 365 Suite 

- Preferable experience with EstimateOne, Buildxact, Wunderbuild, and/or Buildpass 



Essential Qualities:

- Exceptional attention to detail and commitment to accuracy

- Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities

- Ability to work independently while maintaining high standards and being accountable

- Proactive approach to process improvement and efficiency



Work Schedule:

- Part-time (10 hours per week) with potential for increase as the company grows, flexible hours between 7am-5pm AEST

- Preferred start date: 17th March 



Benefits:

- Flexible working arrangements

- Long-term growth potential within the company

- 13th Month Pay: Eligible after completing the probation period (6 months).

- Training: Comprehensive training and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) will be provided. These can then be developed together to suit both parties.

- You'll work closely with the CEO and as the business grows, there will be opportunities to transition to a full-time role, take on additional responsibilities, and eventually lead a team of estimators and administrators yourself.



How to Apply:

Please submit your CV and a cover letter explaining why you are the best fit for this role and what made you apply for it.

Include details about your technical setup (computer, internet speed, etc.), your experience with the software listed above, and any previous experience you think is beneficial to the company. If possible, attach samples of previous work related to estimation.

title: Estimator/Quantity Surveyor
description:
Company: Growing Australian Estimating Consultancy

Hours: 10 hours per week to start (during AEST business hours, set by agreement), with potential to increase





About Us

We're a growing Australian estimating and quantity surveying consultancy working with builders, contractors, and trades across residential and commercial construction. Our team is small, remote-first, and holds its work to a high standard. You'll work directly with the founder — a qualified quantity surveyor — and alongside one other estimator.



This is a genuine part-time role with a mix of estimating and administration work. We need someone who can switch between preparing a cost estimate in the morning and managing
s or updating project records in the afternoon without dropping the ball on either.



You'll review each other's work before it goes to clients, so attention to detail and the confidence to flag errors are essential.



We use AI tools within the business for administrative tasks — drafting
s, organising information, research — and encourage you to do the same where it helps your productivity. AI must not be used for estimating, takeoffs, or any work involving quantities or pricing. You are responsible for the accuracy of everything you submit.



What You'll Be Doing:

Estimating & Technical

- Quantity takeoffs from construction documentation including architectural, structural, civil, mechanical, and electrical drawings

- Preparing cost estimates, bills of quantities, and pricing documents in Excel

- Peer-reviewing estimates produced by other tea
mbers and flagging errors or discrepancies

- Requesting and collating subcontractor and supplier pricing via

- Maintaining organised project files and documentation



Administration & Communication

- Managing and responding to
s on behalf of the business

- Updating project records and task trackers

- Preparing documents, proposals, and general correspondence

- Following up with clients, subcontractors, and suppliers

- Logging time accurately against projects and maintaining task records

- General research and ad hoc tasks as needed



The balance between estimating and admin work will shift depending on workload and business needs.



Software We Use

You'll work across most of the following tools over time. We don't expect experience in all of them, but you need to be adaptable and comfortable learning new software quickly.

- Estimating & takeoff: Cubit, Dindo, Styck, CostX

- Tender management: EstimateOne

- Spreadsheets: Microsoft Excel (non-negotiable — formulas, structured workbooks, formatting)

- Project tracking: TrackingTime, Airtable

- Communication: Gmail, Outlook, Slack





What We're Looking For

Required

- A degree in quantity surveying, construction management, civil engineering, architecture, or a related field

- At least 3 years of experience in construction estimating or quantity surveying

- Ability to read and interpret all types of construction documentation

- Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel — this is non-negotiable

- Excellent written English — you'll be drafting
s and documents on behalf of an Australian business

- Organised and able to manage multiple tasks across different areas without things slipping

- Reliable internet connection and a quiet workspace

- Available to work during Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST) business hours

- Honest and proactive — we'd rather you ask a question than guess

- Comfortable learning new software tools with minimal hand-holding



Preferred

- Experience with Australian construction methods, terminology, or standards (strongly preferred)

- Experience with any of the estimating or takeoff tools listed above

- Experience working remotely for international clients

- Exposure to both residential and commercial project types

- Previous experience managing inboxes or calendars for someone else





What We Offer

- Consistent, ongoing part-time work with a growing company

- Hours during AEST business hours, set by agreement — with flexibility earned over time

- Variety — estimating and administration across multiple clients and project types

- Potential to increase hours and take on more responsibility as the business grows

- Clear processes, templates, and SOPs to support your work

- A founder who understands the technical work and can help you develop

- AI use is encouraged for admin tasks

- 13th month pay

- Payment via Wise in PHP





How to Apply

Please include:

1. A brief introduction — your experience, skills, and what kind of work you enjoy

2. Your resume or CV

3. An example of your work (a sample takeoff, estimate, bill of quantities, or well-structured spreadsheet — feel free to redact client details)

4. Your availability and preferred working hours

5. Which of the software tools listed above you have experience with, and your level of proficiency in each

6. Briefly describe a project where you identified and corrected an error in someone else's estimate or your own. What was the error, and how did you catch it?



Shortlisted candidates will be given a short paid trial covering a mix of estimating and admin tasks.

title: Ads & Content Operator (Meta + Google + Video Editing)
description:
Ads & Content Operator (Meta + Google + Video Editing)



Location: Remote (Philippines)

Hours: Part-Time (10–12 hours per week)

Rate: $8–$12/hour (based on experience)





About the Company



We are a U.S.-based premium wellness and recovery business focused on helping people reduce pain, move better, and improve performance.



Our work is centered around advanced bodywork, movement-based assessments, and recovery technologies such as red light therapy, PEMF, and cryotherapy.



We work with a mix of clients ranging from individuals dealing with pain and postural issues to athletes and high-performing individuals who want to optimize how their body functions.



Our approach is not generic or cookie-cutter. Every session is tailored to the individual, focusing on identifying and addressing the root cause rather than just treating symptoms.



We are not a discount or volume-based business. We focus on quality, education, and long-term results.



In addition to our service-based business, we also operate an e-commerce product line in the wellness and recovery space.







Role Overview



We are looking for someone who can handle the backend execution of advertising and content.



We will provide:

 • Ad copy

 • Messaging

 • Target audience

 • Overall direction



Your role is to execute, manage, and optimize.



This is not a strategy or copywriting role.

You must be strong in Ads Manager, tracking, and execution.







Responsibilities



Ads (Primary Focus)

 • Set up and manage Meta 
/Instagram) ad campaigns

 • Set up and manage Google Ads (Search campaigns)

 • Optimize campaigns based on performance data

 • Manage budgets and basic scaling

 • Set up Meta Pixel and Conversion API (CAPI)

 • Ensure proper tracking for leads (service business) and purchases (e-commerce)







Content & Video Editing (Secondary)

 • Edit short-form videos (Reels, ads, UGC-style content)

 • Add captions, hooks, and clean edits

 • Prepare content for posting





Reporting

 • Provide weekly reports including:

 • Cost per lead / purchase

 • What’s working

 • What’s not working

 • Recommended next steps





Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

 • Experience with Meta Ads Manager

 • Experience with Google Ads (Search campaigns)

 • Experience setting up Pixel and Conversion API

 • Understanding of campaign structure and optimization

 • Basic video editing skills (CapCut, Premiere Pro, or similar)

 • Ability to follow direction and execute efficiently

 • Clear English communication





Nice to Have

 • Experience with lead generation (service-based businesses)

 • Experience with e-commerce ads

 • Experience working with U.S.-based businesses

 • Understanding of high-ticket or premium services





How to Apply (IMPORTANT)



If you do not answer these, your application will not be reviewed.

 1. How would you structure a Meta campaign with a $50/day budget?

 2. How do you set up Meta Pixel and Conversion API?

 3. What do you check first if an ad is not performing?

 4. Share examples of ads you have managed (screenshots preferred)

 5. Share 2–3 videos you have edited







Important



We are not looking for a general VA or beginner marketer.



We are looking for someone who:

 • Understands execution

 • Can manage ads correctly

 • Can edit clean, professional content

 • Can follow direction and improve performance over time



This is a long-term opportunity for the right person.
